content.cn_text: 第三部分。你将听到两名学生 Ruth 和 Edward 讨论他们的作业。
content.en_text: Section 3. You will hear two students called Ruth and Edward discussing their assignment

content.cn_text: 作业主题是肯尼亚一个涉及大象的保护问题。首先，你有一些时间浏览
content.en_text: about a conservation issue involving elephants in Kenya. First, you have some time to look

content.cn_text: 第21至第24题。现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第24题。
content.en_text: at questions 21 to 24. Now, listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.

content.cn_text: Ruth：好的，Edward。我这里有一些我们保护作业的笔记。我看了你提到的
content.en_text: Ruth: Right, Edward. I've got some notes here for our conservation assignment. I had a look at the

content.cn_text: 肯尼亚研究。我之前没意识到农民遇到了这么多问题，
content.en_text: research you mentioned in Kenya. I hadn't realized that farmers were having so many problems with

content.cn_text: 因为那里的大象会吃他们的庄稼。 Edward：是的，问题是当大象进入农民的田地并
content.en_text: elephants eating their crops there. Edward: Yes, the trouble is when elephants get into farmers' fields and

content.cn_text: 毁坏庄稼时，会产生各种其他后果。 Ruth：我想农民也不能直接
content.en_text: destroy their crops, that has all sorts of other results. Ruth: I don't suppose the farmers can just get

content.cn_text: 搬到别的地方去。 Edward：不能，但这是个真正的问题，因为农民会射杀大象，
content.en_text: up and go somewhere else. Edward: No, but it's a real problem because farmers are shooting the elephants

content.cn_text: 以保护庄稼。然后其他大象受到影响，变得对人类非常有敌意。
content.en_text: to keep their crops safe. And then the other elephants are affected and become very hostile to humans.

content.cn_text: 但你也能理解，对农民来说，受到威胁的并不是房屋和家人。
content.en_text: But you can see for the farmers it's not homes and families that have been threatened.

content.cn_text: Ruth：如果自己的生计被毁，农民不想支持大象保护，
content.en_text: Ruth: And it's understandable that farmers don't feel like supporting elephant conservation

content.cn_text: 这是可以理解的。你会觉得自己有权保护财产。 Edward：没错。
content.en_text: if their own livelihood is being destroyed. You feel you have the right to defend your property. Edward: Exactly.

content.cn_text: 所以我想我们的作业可以先讲问题，再介绍
content.en_text: So I thought for our assignment we can start with the problem then introduce the

content.cn_text: 他们一直在试验的解决方案。 Ruth：所以现在说到蜜蜂。我从没想过大象
content.en_text: solutions they've been experimenting with. Ruth: So now we get on to the bees. I never thought elephants

content.cn_text: 会怕蜜蜂。不过我想，即使像大象那么大，被蜜蜂蜇也会疼。
content.en_text: would be afraid of bees. But I suppose even if you're as big as an elephant a bee sting can still hurt.

content.cn_text: Edward：我觉得要看情况。蜜蜂无法刺穿成年大象身体上的厚皮肤，
content.en_text: Edward: Well I think it depends. Bees can't sting through the thick skin on an adult elephant's body

content.cn_text: 但它们会攻击敏感部位，比如眼睛附近。 Ruth：哦，那一定很疼。我听说它们
content.en_text: but they target the sensitive areas like near the eyes. Ruth: Oh that must really hurt and I heard they

content.cn_text: 会直接钻进大象鼻子里并在那里蜇它们。 Edward：是的，还有小象，
content.en_text: get right inside the elephant's trunk and sting there. Edward: Yes and then there are the baby elephants,

content.cn_text: 也就是幼象，它们皮肤更薄，所以可能受到严重影响，尤其是如果它们被
content.en_text: the calves which have thinner skin so they can be really badly affected especially if they get

content.cn_text: 一整群蜜蜂攻击。 Ruth：所以带小象的大象可能会受到蜜蜂威胁，即使它们
content.en_text: attacked by a whole swarm of bees. Ruth: So elephants with babies may be threatened by bees even if they

content.cn_text: 自己没有被蜇。
content.en_text: don't get stung themselves.

content.cn_text: 在听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间浏览第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion you have some time to read questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: 现在请听录音，并回答第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: Edward：好的，接下来我们可以描述基于大象对蜜蜂恐惧的实验。
content.en_text: Edward: Okay, so next we can go on to describe the experiments based on this fear that elephants have

content.cn_text: 其中一种方法是，他们在农场附近的树上挂满装有蜜蜂的容器，
content.en_text: of bees. So there was one way they hung containers full of bees in trees near the farms and they

content.cn_text: 同时也观察没有容器的树，结果发现大象踩倒了那些
content.en_text: listened in trees without containers and they found the elephants trampled down the trees that

content.cn_text: 没有容器的树，并且远离有蜜蜂的树。
content.en_text: didn't have any containers and stayed well away from the ones with the bees in.

content.cn_text: Ruth：好的，那你读过用蜜蜂录音而不是真蜜蜂做实验的文章吗？
content.en_text: Ruth: Okay, so did you read the articles about the experiment where it was done with the audio recording of bees instead

content.cn_text: Edward：没有。 Ruth：他们有一段愤怒蜂群的录音，并播放给
content.en_text: of real bees? Edward: No. Ruth: Well, they've got a recording of a swarm of angry bees and they played it to

content.cn_text: 野外象群听。它们立刻停止正在做的事，站直，
content.en_text: herds of elephants out in the wild and they all stopped what they were doing immediately and stood

content.cn_text: 抬起头，耳朵张开。 Edward：它们一定非常意识到附近有危险。
content.en_text: erect with their heads up and their ears extended. Edward: They must have been really aware there was danger

content.cn_text: Ruth：但那不是它们可以面对并冲过去的危险，所以它们朝
content.en_text: somewhere. Ruth: But not the sort of danger that they can face up to and charge, so they dashed off in the

content.cn_text: 相反方向迅速跑开，并且一直回头看。
content.en_text: opposite direction. They kept turning their heads around.

content.cn_text: Edward：大概是在寻找其实并不存在的蜂群。 Ruth：是的，研究人员测试了18个不同象群，除了
content.en_text: Edward: Presumably looking for the swarm of bees which wasn't there. Ruth: Yes, and researchers tested 18 different herds of the elephants and all but

content.cn_text: 一个象群外，其他都以同样方式反应。 Edward：也许那个象群只是没听清。
content.en_text: one responded in the same way. Edward: Maybe that herd just didn't hear it properly.

content.cn_text: 这似乎只是主要发现中的一个小例外。Ruth: 我觉得研究人员实际上得出结论，认为这个特定象群缺乏
content.en_text: It seems a very small exception to the main finding. Ruth: I think researchers actually concluded that this particular herd lacked

content.cn_text: 任何与蜜蜂相关的经验。它们从没遇到过蜜蜂，所以不害怕录音。 Edward：明白。
content.en_text: any experience of bees. They'd never encountered any so they weren't afraid of the recording. Edward: Right.

content.cn_text: 哦，所以那就是原因。 Ruth：正是。但让研究人员惊讶的是，在
content.en_text: Oh so that was the reason. Ruth: Exactly. But what surprised the researchers was, from the

content.cn_text: 17个听到录音后采取行动的象群中，他们发现几乎50%的象群
content.en_text: 17 herds that did take action when they heard the recording, they found almost 50% of the herds

content.cn_text: 在听到录音后10秒内非常迅速地做出了反应。 Edward：哦，这很有意思。
content.en_text: did this very quickly within 10 seconds of hearing the recording. Edward: Oh that's interesting.

content.cn_text: Ruth: 对。所以播放蜜蜂录音可能是阻止大象的好方法，但事情并不
content.en_text: Ruth: Right. So playing the recordings of bees could be a good deterrent to elephants but it's not all

content.cn_text: 那么简单。 Edward: 是的，你得有电力来运行设备。
content.en_text: that straightforward actually. Edward: Yes, you've got to have the electricity to run the machinery.

content.cn_text: Ruth: 不，这个没问题。它可以用电池运行，但整个装置对电源插座来说相当困难，
content.en_text: Ruth: No, that's okay. It can run on batteries, but the whole thing's quite difficult for electrical outlet

content.cn_text: 可能还算容易，之后还得有人把它全部布置好并确保它正常工作。
content.en_text: which might be easy and then someone's got to set it all out and make sure it's working.

content.cn_text: 这并不难，但确实是一项工作。
content.en_text: That's not difficult but it's really a job.

content.cn_text: Edward: 他们也在尝试其他方法，比如用电栅栏让大象远离庄稼。这个效果不太成功，但当他们
content.en_text: Edward: They're trying other methods as well like electric fences to keep the elephants away from the crops. They haven't had much success but when they

content.cn_text: 在栅栏上挂牛铃时，似乎能把它们吓走。
content.en_text: hang cowbells on the fences that seemed to scare them off.

content.cn_text: Ruth: 是的，我读到他们也试过用辣椒作为威慑，但那完全没有吓到大象。 Edward: 对，所以我觉得我们已经有很多
content.en_text: Ruth: Yes, I've read they tried chili peppers as a deterrent too but that didn't scare the elephants at all. Edward: Right, so I think we've got plenty

content.cn_text: 信息了。
content.en_text: of information.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。你现在有半分钟时间检查答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 3. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

groups.desc:
Choose TWO letters A-E.
What are the TWO results happened as elephants stepping on farmer's field?
groups.title: Questions 21-22.

list.title: A
list.content: farmers moved away from the affected areas

list.title: C
list.content: farmers have less support for elephant conservation

list.title: D
list.content: farmers tried to protect their families

list.title: E
list.content: elephants became aggressive towards the farmers

items.title: What are the TWO results happened as elephants stepping on farmer's field?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题为多选题，问大象进入农民田地造成的结果之一。原文说其他大象 affected and become hostile to humans，对应选项 E. elephants became aggressive towards the farmers。

items.title: What are the TWO results happened as elephants stepping on farmer's field?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题为多选题，问大象进入田地造成的另一个结果。原文说农民不愿支持 elephant conservation，所以答案包含 C. farmers have less support for elephant conservation。homes and families 被明确否定。

groups.desc:
Choose TWO letters A-E
What TWO impacts could be made by bees to elephants?
groups.title: Questions 23-24.

list.title: A
list.content: they can hurt the skin of the adult elephants

list.title: B
list.content: they like to attack the sensitive parts of the elephants

list.title: C
list.content: elephants are not afraid of bees

list.title: E
list.content: they have little effect on elephants

items.title: What TWO impacts could be made by bees to elephants?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题为多选题，问蜜蜂对大象的影响之一。原文说蜜蜂会 target the sensitive areas like near the eyes，所以答案包含 B. they like to attack the sensitive parts of the elephants。成年大象厚皮肤不能被刺穿，因此 A 不对。

items.title: What TWO impacts could be made by bees to elephants?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题为多选题，问蜜蜂对大象的另一项影响。原文说 baby elephants/calves 皮肤更薄，can be really badly affected，所以答案包含 D. young elephants are affected negatively。

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter A, B or C
groups.title: Questions 25-30.

items.title: What is result of the experiment of hanging a container of bees by the farms?

options.title: A
options.content: the trees with bees were trampled down by elephants

options.title: B
options.content: the elephants started to look for the trees with bees

options.title: C
options.content: it can be used for protecting farmer's crops

items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问在农场附近悬挂蜜蜂容器实验的结果。原文说大象远离有蜜蜂的树，因此这种方式可用于保护农民庄稼，所以答案是 C. it can be used for protecting farmer's crops。

items.title: How did the elephants respond to the audio recording of bees?

options.title: A
options.content: they were not aware of the danger

options.title: B
options.content: they ran away quickly and kept looking back

options.title: C
options.content: they stopped and looked for the source of the sound

items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问大象对蜜蜂录音如何反应。原文说它们 dashed off in the opposite direction，并 kept turning their heads around，所以答案是 B. they ran away quickly and kept looking back。

items.title: The reason why one herd of elephants didn't run away

options.title: A
options.content: they didn't hear the sound clear

options.title: B
options.content: they never had the experience before

options.title: C
options.content: they didn't pay attention to the recording

items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问为什么有一群大象没有逃跑。原文说它们 lacked any experience of bees，从未遇到过蜜蜂，所以答案是 B. they never had the experience before。没听清只是 Edward 的猜测，被后面解释取代。

items.title: What surprised the researchers when elephants heard the recording?

options.title: A
options.content: some elephants reacted to it immediately

options.title: B
options.content: they were scared for only 10 seconds

options.title: C
options.content: most elephants didn't take any action

items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问研究人员对大象听到录音后的什么反应感到惊讶。原文说 almost 50% of the herds did this very quickly within 10 seconds，所以答案是 A. some elephants reacted to it immediately。

items.title: What is the drawback of the application of bee recording?

options.title: A
options.content: finding suitable power to run the device

options.title: B
options.content: training suitable people to set out the device

options.title: C
options.content: the battery can't last long time

items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问应用蜜蜂录音的缺点是什么。原文说电力问题可以用电池解决，真正麻烦的是需要有人 set it all out and make sure it's working，所以答案是 B. training suitable people to set out the device。

items.title: What is the other effective method as a deterrent to elephants?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问另一种有效的大象威慑方法是什么。原文说 electric fences 不太成功，但在栅栏上 hanging cowbells seemed to scare them off，所以答案是 A. hanging cowbells。chili peppers 没有效果。
